executive_summary: Conflict intensity remains at MAJOR level with confirmed loss of a US KC-135 refueling aircraft in Iraq and death of a French soldier in Erbil. Iran-aligned forces continue high-volume missile barrages against Israel and Gulf states, while unverified claims emerge regarding significant damage to a US carrier group. Economic disruption is expanding with regional market volatility and supply chain precautions.
whats_new: US aircraft type confirmed as KC-135 with 6 personnel on board (3 sources).
French casualty identified as Adjutant-Chef Arnaud Frieon following Macron confirmation (3 sources).
New unverified claim of US carrier group damage and retreat (Al Masirah TV English).
Contradictory signals on Strait of Hormuz closure: Supreme Leader order reported vs. UN envoy denial (CNBC, The Hindu).
confirmed_events: US KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq; crew casualties confirmed by US Central Command and media reports (3 sources).
French soldier killed in drone strike on base near Erbil; attack attributed to Iranian forces (3 sources).
IRGC launched 44th missile wave targeting Israeli cities and US bases in the region (2 sources).
Gulf states (UAE, Qatar, Bahrain) successfully intercepted ballistic missiles and drones targeting their airspace (3 sources).
Missile strike in Zarzir, Israel, destroyed residential structures seven meters from homes (2 sources).
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims Abraham Lincoln carrier group sustained significant damage and is retreating to the US (Al Masirah TV English).
Reports of Supreme Leader order to close Strait of Hormuz contradict UN envoy statement denying closure plans (CNBC, The Hindu).
Iranian drone strike on Salalah Port, Oman, damaged fuel storage tanks (Arab League condemnation).
Ballistic missile observed flying over Incirlik Air Base, Turkey, threatening US assets (War Monitors).

people_travel: UAE cancelled Class 10 and 12 board exams for Indian students due to security concerns (Times of India).
Air raid sirens activated in multiple northern Israeli cities including Kiryat and Acre (Jerusalem Post).
supply_chain: Indian households stockpiling electric stoves amid fears of LPG shortage linked to conflict (South China Morning Post).
Fuel storage tanks damaged at Salalah Port, Oman, following drone strike (Arab League).
market_macro: Asia-Pacific markets tumbled as investors brace for prolonged Middle East war (CNBC).
US defense officials report $11.3bn war cost in first six days of conflict (The Guardian).

outlook_24_72h.base_case: High-intensity exchanges will continue with sustained missile and drone attacks from Iran-aligned groups against Israel and US assets. Regional air defense systems will remain active across the Gulf and Levant.
escalation_risks: Unverified carrier damage claims could trigger direct US naval retaliation.
Potential closure of Strait of Hormuz would severely impact global energy supplies.
Ground invasion warnings suggest risk of expanded US troop deployment.
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: UN Security Council Resolution 2817 condemns Iranian attacks, offering diplomatic pressure. Internal US discussions on exit plans may signal future policy shifts.
